target_behavior.notes: Broad upward bend resembling a section of a large donut.
Rotates pipe outward and slightly upward away from center.
Smooth tapered transition near final section.

design_notes: Continuous internal flow passage required.
Maintain tangent continuity between all sections.
Spiral radius gradually reduces as the pipe rises.
Bends must not look angular or segmented.

draft.rationale: The previous attempt failed due to a high curvature peak at the second waypoint (relative [20, 30, 20]). Following the curvature_repair_hint, I have added two smoothing waypoints from the allowed numeric enum—[12.5, 15, 15] and [10, 35, 25]—to distribute the direction change more gradually while preserving all four required goal waypoints in their original order. The waypoint frame remains relative_to_target to simplify the implementation of the spiral rise.

issues.message: A curved/tapered envelope may intersect; FreeCAD Boolean evidence is authoritative.

draft.rationale: Satisfying the first goal 'straight_start' by creating a 40mm straight pipe segment along the +X direction from the starting port.

observations.message: A curved/tapered envelope may intersect; FreeCAD Boolean evidence is authoritative.

draft.rationale: Satisfying the rising_spiral goal by implementing a spline route starting from port M2.out. The waypoints are copied exactly from the goal requirement using the relative_to_target frame. The final_tangent is authored to follow the direction vector from the penultimate to the terminal waypoint ([0, -25, 10]), approximating the unit vector using available schema literals.

M3.curvature_repair_hint: The curvature peak is near authored path point index 1. Preserve required anchors but spread this direction change over additional, well-separated waypoints before and after it.
